audit_items.proof_trace: The c2ast function allocates List and Map objects via SASS_MEMORY_NEW before recursively processing their elements. If a nested element (e.g., an error value) triggers an exception via the error() function, the allocated container pointer is lost, causing a memory leak. No RAII wrapper or cleanup exists on this error path.
audit_items.attack_scenario: A custom C function returns a Sass list containing an error value. When libsass converts the value via c2ast, the recursion encounters the error, throws an exception, and leaks the memory of the partially constructed list.
